skill.name: dependency-upgrade
skill.description: Plan and execute major dependency upgrades with compatibility checks, staged rollout steps, testing guidance, and rollback planning.
skill.summary: Guide dependency upgrades with staged plans, compatibility checks, tests, and rollback steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but the file is a Markdown dependency-upgrade guide rather than executable skill code. The command and file-write examples are legitimate for the skill domain, but they can change project files, install packages, and fetch remote content if followed without review. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or actual weak cryptographic implementation was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Commands Can Modify Project State
medium_findings.description: The guide recommends npm, yarn, npx, git, and package installation commands. These are normal for dependency upgrades, but they can change package manifests, lock files, branches, and installed code if executed without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly visible in Markdown examples and would alter dependencies or git state if run. They fit the skill purpose, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Remote Content and Tool Invocation Require Review
medium_findings.description: The guide includes a curl request to a GitHub changelog and npx codemod commands. These are legitimate upgrade workflows, but they depend on remote content and downloaded tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network URL and npx commands are directly present. No exfiltration is shown, but remote retrieval and tool execution are real supply-chain considerations.

medium_findings.title: Migration Script Example Performs File Writes
medium_findings.description: The sample migration script reads matching source files and writes modified content back to disk. This is useful for codemods, but it can overwrite source files if used without backups or review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fs.readFileSync and fs.writeFileSync calls are explicit and write to files returned by a glob. The example is benign but has real filesystem impact.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and command examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md contains documentation examples, not executable Ruby code or hidden command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows fenced Markdown examples and ordinary inline paths. There is no Ruby runtime code or dynamic command substitution in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match words inside prose, headings, Markdown filenames, and JavaScript test names. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found no cryptographic API or weak algorithm use. The flagged lines are dependency-upgrade text, test descriptions, or resource names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Installation and Audit Fix Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples include commands that install packages, update package metadata, or automatically apply audit fixes. Users should inspect diffs and lockfile changes before accepting results.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and expected for dependency work. The risk comes from package supply-chain effects and local file changes.

dangerous_patterns.title: File-Rewriting Migration Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample script writes modified content back into source files. This pattern should be run only on a branch with reviewable diffs and backups.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write calls are direct and easy to verify. The pattern is common for codemods but can damage files when applied broadly.

content.user_title: Upgrade Dependencies with Safer Migration Plans
content.value_statement: Major dependency upgrades can break builds, tests, and runtime behavior. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan staged upgrades with compatibility checks, test gates, and rollback steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains semantic versioning and how major, minor, and patch updates affect upgrade risk.
Provides npm, yarn, and npx commands for auditing, listing, deduplicating, and upgrading dependencies.
Shows staged upgrade planning with baseline tests, incremental installs, and repeated validation.
Gives examples for codemods, custom migration scripts, and compatibility tests.
Includes Renovate, Dependabot, rollback, lockfile, peer dependency, and workspace upgrade guidance.
limitations: The skill is a Markdown guide and does not include executable automation in the scanned file.
Commands are JavaScript package-manager focused and may need adaptation for other ecosystems.
Remote changelogs, npx tools, and package releases must be reviewed before use.
The report only scanned SKILL.md, so referenced resource files were not verified here.

use_cases.title: Plan a Framework Major Upgrade
use_cases.description: Create a staged plan for moving frameworks such as React across major versions while preserving build and test confidence.

use_cases.title: Reduce Vulnerable Dependency Risk
use_cases.description: Audit dependency findings, identify safe update paths, and separate patch fixes from major migrations that need review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Upgrade Automation
use_cases.description: Configure Dependabot or Renovate policies that automate low-risk updates and route major upgrades through manual review.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dependency-upgrade skill to review my project dependencies. Identify outdated packages, risky major upgrades, and the safest first checks to run.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Staged Upgrade Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dependency-upgrade skill to plan a staged upgrade from [current versions] to [target versions]. Include upgrade order, test gates, and rollback checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Breaking Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dependency-upgrade skill to analyze breaking changes for [package or framework]. Propose code changes, codemods, and compatibility tests before installation.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Automation Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dependency-upgrade skill to design Renovate or Dependabot rules for this repository. Separate patch, minor, and major update handling with review requirements.

output_examples.input: Upgrade React and related testing libraries in a production app.
output: A dependency inventory grouped by React, routing, testing, and build tooling.
A staged order that upgrades one major component at a time.
Validation steps covering unit tests, integration tests, build checks, and rollback readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a policy for automated dependency updates.
output: A recommended automation schedule and reviewer model.
Automerge rules for low-risk updates and manual review for major updates.
Monitoring and documentation tasks after upgrade pull requests merge.

output_examples.input: Resolve peer dependency conflicts after a package upgrade.
output: A compatibility matrix for the conflicting packages.
Candidate version ranges that satisfy peer requirements.
A test plan to confirm the selected version set works in the project.

best_practices: Run baseline tests and create a reviewable branch before changing dependency versions.
Upgrade one major dependency at a time and inspect package and lockfile diffs after each step.
Read changelogs and migration notes before running codemods or remote tooling.
anti_patterns: Do not upgrade every dependency at once when major versions or peer dependencies are involved.
Do not run npx tools, curl commands, or file-writing scripts without checking their source and expected changes.
Do not ignore peer dependency warnings or force installs without documenting the compatibility risk.

faq.question: Does this skill execute dependency upgrades automatically?
faq.answer: No. The scanned file is a Markdown guide with commands and examples that a user or agent may choose to run.

faq.question: Which package managers does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on npm and yarn workflows, with examples for npm audit, npm-check-updates, Renovate, and Dependabot.

faq.question: Can it help with major framework upgrades?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes staged planning, compatibility checks, breaking change review, codemods, tests, and rollback steps.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium risk warning. The commands are legitimate but can modify files, install packages, or fetch remote content.

faq.question: Does it include prompt injection text?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md. The file does not contain instructions to override system behavior or skip security review.

faq.question: What should users review before applying advice?
faq.answer: Users should review changelogs, package sources, lockfile diffs, generated code changes, test results, and rollback plans.
